mnemonics.title: SQL Command Categories
mnemonics.content: DID: DDL (Define), DML (Manipulate), DCL (Control) - Think 'Data In Different ways'

mnemonics.title: SQL Clauses Order
mnemonics.content: F-W-G-H-O-S: FROM-WHERE-GROUP BY-HAVING-ORDER BY-SELECT - Remember 'Friends Will Give Hugs On Special occasions'

mnemonics.title: Data Independence Types
mnemonics.content: P-LOG: Physical then Logical - Think 'Physical Level first, then Logical'

acronyms.usage: CREATE, ALTER, DROP commands that define database structure
INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE commands that manipulate data
GRANT, REVOKE commands for security and access control
COMMIT, ROLLBACK, SAVEPOINT for transaction management
SELECT command for retrieving data from database
Standard language for managing relational databases
Text-based interface for database interaction (e.g., SQL*Plus)
Visual interface for database operations (e.g., SQL Developer)

visualTips.visualization: Draw three boxes: External (User view) → Conceptual (Logical) → Internal (Physical). Show arrows between them representing data independence.
Create a pie chart showing DDL (20%), DML (40%), DCL (10%), TCL (10%), DQL (20%) of SQL operations.
Flowchart: FROM → WHERE → GROUP BY → HAVING → SELECT → ORDER BY - shows data flow through query processing.
Pyramid diagram: DBA at top, Application Developers middle, End Users at base with different language interfaces.

analogies.analogy: Schema is like a blueprint of a building - it defines the structure, rooms, and how everything is arranged before construction.
A database view is like a virtual window - you can see through it, but it doesn't exist as a physical structure; it reflects what's behind it.
A stored procedure is like a recipe card in a kitchen - once written, anyone can use it repeatedly to cook the same dish without knowing the detailed steps.
A trigger is like a motion sensor light - it automatically turns on (executes) when specific movement (database event) is detected.
A transaction is like a bank transfer - either the entire transfer happens (COMMIT) or nothing happens (ROLLBACK); partial transfers are not allowed.
